subject: [PATCH v3 0/3] mm: handle device-private PMDs in walk callbacks

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton noted that the patch does not include hotfixes for pre-existing issues, and instead suggests adding a Fixes: commit to address these potential problems.
reviews.raw_body:
Added, thanks.

I didn't add these as hotfixes - the Fixes: commit is somewhat old. 
Feel free to disagree with this!

Sashiko went nuts over possible pre-existing issues:

	https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260710105557.1987433-1-usama.arif@linux.dev

2026-07-10.patch_summary: This patch series addresses an issue where device-private PMDs (Page Map Directory entries) are not handled correctly in certain walk callbacks, leading to potential bugs or crashes. The problem arises from the fact that these PMDs can be present even when they don't hold a valid huge page mapping. The patches modify several MM functions to skip over non-present PMDs and handle device-private PMDs accordingly, ensuring correct behavior and preventing potential issues.

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary:
Gave Reviewed-by

Reviewer Gregory Price questioned whether softleaf entries should be warned on, suggesting that all three patches could be simplified by introducing a pmd_is_softleaf() function if such entries are not wanted to trigger warnings.
reviews.raw_body:
I just realized, are there softleaf entries we wouldn't want to WARN on?
If not, should all three of these patches just be something like
pmd_is_softleaf()?

x_is_softleaf() does not exist, just curious if it should, and not
worth holding up the patch.

~Gregory

reviews.author: Balbir Singh
reviews.summary: Reviewer Balbir Singh found the patch's approach plausible and is working on additional tests, but no specific technical issue or suggestion was raised.
reviews.raw_body:
The pointed out seems plausible? I am working on some tests to catch
more errors via hmm-tests.

Balbir
